label: Sentry
description: Connect to Sentry to query error events, issues, performance traces, and alerts from your projects.

form.title: Connect Sentry
form.subtitle: Paste your Sentry auth token to give Anton access to your projects and error data.

methods.label: Auth Token
methods.description: Paste a Sentry user auth token (sntrys_…) from Settings → Auth Tokens. Works for both Sentry.io and self-hosted instances. Simplest and fastest setup.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Sentry Auth Token

### Steps

1. Log in to [Sentry.io](https://sentry.io) (or your self-hosted Sentry instance).
2. Click your avatar in the top-right corner, then go to **Settings → Auth Tokens** (direct URL: `https://sentry.io/settings/account/api/auth-tokens/`).
3. Click **Create New Token**.
4. Give the token a name like `Anton`.
5. Select the scopes Anton needs — at minimum: `project:read`, `org:read`, `event:read`, `issue:read`. Add `event:write` or `issue:write` if you want Anton to resolve or assign issues.
6. Click **Create Token**.
7. Copy the token (it starts with `sntrys_`). **It is shown only once** — store it somewhere safe before closing.
8. Paste it into the **Auth Token** field below.

### Self-hosted Sentry

If you run your own Sentry instance, fill in the **Base URL** field with your instance's root URL (e.g. `https://sentry.mycompany.com`). Leave it blank to use Sentry.io.

### Notes

- Tokens are scoped — they only grant the permissions you selected.
- Revoke tokens any time from **Settings → Auth Tokens** in Sentry.
- Organization-level tokens (for CI/automation) are separate from user tokens; either works with the same field.

fields.name: auth_token
fields.label: Auth Token
fields.description: User auth token from Sentry Settings → Auth Tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Leave as-is for Sentry.io. Change to your self-hosted instance root URL if applicable (e.g. https://sentry.mycompany.com).

fields.name: organization_slug
fields.label: Organization Slug
fields.description: Your Sentry organization slug — visible in the URL after sentry.io/organizations/. Required for some API calls; Anton can often infer it automatically.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Sentry App)
methods.description: Use a custom Sentry OAuth application (client ID + secret) to authorize via browser. Requires creating a Sentry App in your organization settings.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 via Sentry App

This method requires creating a custom Sentry App in your organization. Use Auth Token instead unless you have a specific reason to use OAuth.

### Steps

1. Log in to Sentry and go to **Settings → Developer Settings → New Internal Integration** (or **New Sentry App** for public apps).
2. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton handles the local redirect).
3. Under **Permissions**, grant at minimum: `Project: Read`, `Organization: Read`, `Event: Read`, `Issue & Event: Read`.
4. Enable **OAuth** and save the app.
5. From the app's detail page, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
6. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit**.
7. Anton opens a browser window to Sentry's consent screen — approve access.

### Notes

- OAuth tokens expire and require refresh; Anton handles this automatically.
- Self-hosted Sentry may have a different OAuth base URL — check your instance docs.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your Sentry App detail page under Settings → Developer Settings.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; found on the same Sentry App detail page.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Leave as-is for Sentry.io. For self-hosted Sentry, enter your instance root URL.
